response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and backend software engineer with strong web, mobile, and embedded software experience, but his background is almost entirely disconnected from the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, hardware-software co-design, and deep neural network optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby and C/C++ work at National Instruments show some systems-level awareness, but these fall far short of the hardware architecture and deep-learning workload proficiency required at the principal level.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency and high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O, fibers, and caching in DLang)
Broad systems breadth spanning embedded, C/C++, and hardware-adjacent work (NI LabWindows, robotic arms)
Current AI-adjacent work on agentic infrastructure and MCP frameworks, indicating growing exposure to AI tooling
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning workloads, neural network training/inference optimization, or SIMD/MIMD architectures
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack/DevOps engineering, with no indication of a transition toward hardware or performance architecture for AI systems

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No deep learning or AI accelerator workload expertise
Low-latency systems and performance optimization background
